step_1_context.text: Time expressions that refer to the present, such as this morning / week / month and today, can be used with either past simple or present perfect verbs.

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: Time expressions that refer to the present, such as this morning / week / month and today, can be used with either past simple or present perfect verbs.
step_2_explanation.formula:
In a sentence which includes a time clause with since, we generally prefer a past simple verb in the time clause (referring to a particular point in the past) and a present perfect verb in the main clause:
 • Since Mr Dodson became president unemployment has increased.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: With time clauses introduced by after, when, until, as soon as, once, by the time and the time expressions the minute / second / moment the past simple refers to past, completed events and the present perfect refers to future events.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 3.1.1 A: Shall I make us some dinner? ... B: No, thanks. I ___ to the dentist this afternoon and my mouth hurts too much to eat anything. (go)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The period of the afternoon is fully completed in the speaker's mind.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 3.1.2 I ___ three lectures today and I still have two more later this afternoon. (have)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The period 'today' includes the present moment, and more lectures are expected.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 3.1.3 It was so hot today that I ___ shorts and a T-shirt at work. (wear)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The work day is over, even though it's still 'today'.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 3.1.4 We ___ £400 on food this month and there's another week to go before I get paid. (spend)
3.1.5 A: Do you want a lift home? B: No, I ___ this morning because my alarm clock didn't go off, so I need to work late. (oversleep)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The 'morning' is definitely over as they are leaving work now.
